title: Jira Service Management Connector
author: Weves
body:
Pull in all tickets from a specified [Jira Service Management](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/service-management) project. 

Checkout the connector creation README here for more details on the best way to add new connectors: https://github.com/danswer-ai/danswer/blob/main/backend/danswer/connectors/README.md.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the connector creation guidelines in the provided README and ensure proper handling of Jira API pagination and authentication mechanisms.

title: Replace ModemManager with modem.py
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Let's replace ModemManager with ~200 lines of Python. Faster boot times, better [reliability](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/b27fa58444766c47f1ef980af79d324a669012c4/system/hardware/hardwared.py#L135), and tinier AGNOS.

---

Bounty targets
* All of openpilot works
* Works on both comma 3X and comma four
* At least 10 Discord users run it for a ~week and sign off on it, both with prime and non-prime service

Misc notes:
* comma 3X uses [Quectel EG25](https://www.quectel.com/product/lte-eg25-g/)
* comma four uses [Quectl EG916](https://www.quectel.com/product/lte-cat-1-bis-eg916q-gl/)
* Let's try not to make the comma 3X and comma four code paths too different if possible
  * comma 3X currently uses QMI, but I think we can get away with just AT commands
* routing priority still needs to work properly (e.g. WiFi > LTE when both are up)
* We'll need to replace dbus, maybe just write a dumb state file to `/dev/shm/modem_state.txt`?

**Bonus target ($500):** `ping -c 1 google.com` works <1 min after powering up a comma four with comma prime.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Focus on implementing AT command handling for both Quectel modems and ensure routing priority logic is preserved while replacing dbus with a lightweight state management approach like a shared memory file.

title: Setup proper pip package
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
`pandacan` is already a pip package, but it doesn't include the FW and some other things to fully use the panda project.

The package should be sufficient to remove panda as a submodule from [openpilot](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot) and add it as a normal Python dependency to the pyproject.toml. For validation, make an openpilot PR that does this and ensure CI passes.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Ensure the pip package includes all necessary firmware files and dependencies, and update pyproject.toml in openpilot to reference the new package for CI validation.

title: Enable 100% branch coverage check
author: adeebshihadeh
body: Your goal is to [enable](https://github.com/commaai/opendbc/pull/2555) the branch coverage check and carefully fix all the violations. For each violation: 1. can we remove this branch? 2. can we rewrite this branch? 3. add test coverage Our test coverage should be fairly complete, so this should mostly be removing and rewriting. Here's where we're at now:

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Focus on analyzing branches with low coverage using the coverage report and prioritize removing or rewriting redundant logic before adding tests.

title: [$100 bounty] Make a high quality video walkthrough
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Make a video walkthrough of flash.comma.ai, then make a PR to embed it onto the site.

Good example videos:
* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NAz5NGnaDKs&list=PLawfivpGQ-voT2siiz61hgVhdhtfy2y0P
* https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNnRmEyVSVQ&t=89s

Must be high quality!

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Create a professional-quality video walkthrough of flash.comma.ai, ensuring clear visuals and audio, and submit a PR embedding the video on the site as per the examples provided.

title: [$500 Bounty] Refactor panda to use source + header file structure
author: robbederks
body:
It's time we move the repo from the current style (everything is a header file) to having separate source and header files. This should clean up all the import orders and `*_definitions.h` files, as well as make it a lot easier to add new drivers etc.

Bounty for a clean PR with minimal changes.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Focus on reorganizing files by separating logic into `.c` source files and `.h` header files, ensuring proper dependency management and minimal changes to existing functionality.

title: cppcheck: catch ints as bools
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Context in #1876

This is three steps:
1. add support for this to cppcheck
2. upstream that support to cppcheck
3. PR to enable it and fix all the violations

While we're waiting for them to merge the support upstream, you can open a draft PR using the patch.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Implement a custom cppcheck rule to detect implicit conversions of integers to booleans, test it locally, and prepare a patch for upstream submission.

title: Get MetaDrive simulator working on macOS
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Must work as well as on Linux.

At a minimum, this means it must pass the CI test: https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/tools/sim/tests/test_metadrive_bridge.py.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Ensure macOS compatibility by debugging dependencies and environment setup in test_metadrive_bridge.py, focusing on any Linux-specific assumptions or hardcoded paths.

title: Speedup system build time
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Goal is to get a <10m build time without a cache on the CI runner. According to https://github.com/commaai/agnos-builder/pull/251, we're currently at 33m. 

For validation, trigger CI 5 times and post the timings of the "[Build system](https://github.com/commaai/agnos-builder/blob/0480a887226779d3f5f1f8394bfaa1afb78f6be0/.github/workflows/build.yaml#L74)" step. The max shouldn't exceed 10m.


1. remove anything that's unnecessary
2. refactoring and reordering steps
3. parallelize steps


If done carefully, it should be possible to do this without a comma 3/3X and have someone on the Discord test it for you at the end.


Bounty is worth 3x ($600) if it ends up being <5m.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Analyze the build.yaml file to identify redundant steps, optimize dependency installation, and implement parallelization using GitHub Actions' 'matrix' strategy.

title: jungle: support replaying CAN messages from an SD card
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
[panda jungle v2](https://comma.ai/shop/panda-jungle) has an SD card slot. The goal is to use that SD card to replay CAN messages from an openpilot route instead of having to run [can_replay.py](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/tools/replay/can_replay.py).

The on-disk format doesn't matter; we just need an easy script to "provision" the SD card.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Implement a function in the Panda firmware to read CAN messages from the SD card in a predefined format and replay them, leveraging existing CAN transmission logic.

title: Improve pytest collection time to <1s
author: sshane
body:
Currently it takes ~6-7s with these plugins:

```
platform linux -- Python 3.11.4, pytest-8.2.1, pluggy-1.5.0
benchmark: 4.0.0 (defaults: timer=time.perf_counter disable_gc=False min_rounds=5 min_time=0.000005 max_time=1.0 calibration_precision=10 warmup=False warmup_iterations=100000)
Using --randomly-seed=3123610973
rootdir: /home/batman/openpilot
configfile: pyproject.toml
testpaths: tools/, common/, system/, selfdrive/
plugins: anyio-4.3.0, timeout-2.3.1, xdist-3.6.1, cpp-2.5.0, cov-5.0.0, mock-3.14.0, forked-1.6.0, dash-2.11.1, benchmark-4.0.0, randomly-3.15.0, subtests-0.12.1, flaky-3.8.1, hypothesis-6.47.5, asyncio-0.23.7, nbmake-1.5.3, repeat-0.9.3
asyncio: mode=Mode.STRICT

============================================================================= 4011 tests collected in 6.46s ==============================================================================

real    0m8.935s
user    0m10.833s
sys     0m2.163s
```


Verification:

Run `time pytest --collect-only` in the root openpilot directory and post both the `real` time and the collection time as reported by pytest (`=== 4011 tests collected in 6.61s ===`). Include the times before and after your PR.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Optimize pytest configuration by reducing unnecessary plugins and test discovery paths, and consider using pytest's `--disable-warnings` and `--cache-clear` options to streamline collection.

title: Reduce AGNOS system image size to <1GB
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Convenience things, like `valgrind`, should be the last  to go. First up is anything we're just wasting space on, like package caches or AGNOS build-time dependencies.

If we have to start cutting these things, I think we'll want to setup an "AGNOS dev" package that will install all those extras together.

```
batman:output$ du -hs system.img
4.3G    system.img
```

`scripts/check_space.sh` is a nice tool for debugging this and will allow you to poke around like this:
![image](https://github.com/commaai/agnos-builder/assets/8762862/d3b9cafc-3dea-496f-ab17-c404e0dccabe)

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Use `scripts/check_space.sh` to identify and remove unnecessary build-time dependencies and package caches, then consider creating an 'AGNOS dev' package for optional extras.

title: test_models: add a test that fuzzes the tx messages
author: sshane
body:
Currently we compare the car states from panda and openpilot to ensure that both parse CAN messages with the same 1) validity checks and 2) correctness/no bugs. That let us fix quite a few mismatches in behavior between the two: https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/pull/30443

Now we want to fuzz the tx messages as well, to discover bugs related to controlsAllowed in panda, and general tx safety. Here is one such mismatch that we should aim to catch: https://github.com/commaai/panda/pull/1948

See the first PR linked above for rx to know where to start. Bounty is for a merged PR that adds fuzz testing for sent openpilot messages that
1) detects mismatches in tx (sent messages) logic between openpilot and panda,
1) extensible (generic, and easy to expand with more state),
2) as performant as our current fuzzy testing, and
3) covers as many cases as possible (catches a few bugs along the way, if there are any).

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the existing fuzzing implementation for rx messages in the linked PR and adapt it to target tx message logic, ensuring compatibility with both openpilot and panda's safety checks.

title: Ship Ubuntu 24.04 + mainline kernel to master
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Now that the initial 24.04 work is done, two hard parts remain: mainlining the kernel and doing the validation to get both shipped to master.

`test_onroad` is a good litmus test for functionality while developing. Once it's in a state where the test passes reliably on a single device, I can deploy it to to several devices in our internal testing closet. 

Locked to @andiradulescu, who did the initial 24.04 work.

Bounty is split into two parts:
- [x] Get 24.04 shipped to master ($1k)
- [ ] Get mainline kernel shipped to master ($2k)

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Requires expertise in AT Commands and Linux Networking (Route Metrics). The key is to strip ModemManager to ~200 lines of Python. Bounty requires 10+ Discord users to sign off.

title: Match stock Hyundai button logic
author: sshane
body:
Currently we diverge from the original behavior when openpilot (alpha) longitudinal is used, which has confused some users in the past having them ask on Discord how to engage.

We should match the stock longitudinal button logic in panda and openpilot, here's what we don't match:
- [ ] CAN cars with set, resume, and main on button
  - [ ] Match main on button, which is a grate on whether cruise is "available". For example, pressing it has no effect on availability, nor resetting cruise speed, etc. 
  - [ ] Resume should not be allowed if no cruise speed set (immediately after toggling main/availability)
- [ ] CAN and CAN FD cars with set, [pause/resume](https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/assets/25857203/5402ad78-2599-4232-b673-aed62a8fb270), and main on button that toggles cruise:
  - [ ] Above criteria for main button, in addition to enabling cruise on rising edge the first time.
  - [ ] Pause/resume button should toggle cruise on and off if cruise is available.
- [ ] Not all HKG cars have this main and pause/resume button functionality and this button shows up as the normal resume button to openpilot, so we ideally need a way to differentiate these buttons/cars.

This needs to be done for both Hyundai's CAN and CAN FD platforms.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Review the Hyundai-specific logic in `selfdrive/car/hyundai` and `panda/board/safety/safety_hyundai.h` to align button behavior with stock functionality, focusing on differentiating CAN and CAN FD signals for main and pause/resume buttons.

title: Reduce kernel boot time to <1s on comma 3X
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
`systemd-analyze` reports this for the latest AGNOS:
```
Startup finished in 3.650s (kernel) + 7.343s (userspace) = 10.994s
graphical.target reached after 7.059s in userspace
```

Get the kernel part of that down to <1s in a self-contained way that doesn't delay the rest of the openpilot.
* can't just defer stuff to later and cost boot time at later stages
* can turn off stuff openpilot doesn't use (NFC, bluetooth, etc.)

AGNOS is built with https://github.com/commaai/agnos-builder

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Analyze the AGNOS kernel configuration in the agnos-builder repository to disable unnecessary modules (e.g., NFC, Bluetooth) and optimize boot parameters using tools like `systemd-analyze` and `dmesg` for bottleneck identification.

title: Run MetaDrive simulation test in GitHub Actions
author: adeebshihadeh
body:
Requirements:
* runs on the free for open source Actions instances (can't use a higher tier one)
* reliable, passes 20+ times in a row
* runs in real time: 1m of driving in openpilot is equivalent to 1m in CI time
* logs (qlog, rlog, and camera files) uploaded as an artifact

https://github.com/commaai/openpilot/blob/master/tools/sim/tests/test_sim_bridge.py

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Ensure the MetaDrive simulation runs deterministically by mocking any external dependencies and verify compatibility with GitHub Actions' resource constraints.

title: [$200 bounty] [Ford Q4] Ford Lightnings Harness Causes Errors When Openpilot Not Running
author: coffee-cake-isaac
body:
### Describe the bug

There's currently an issue with F150 Lightnings on the Ford Q4 Harness/CAN-FD platform. This is specific to the Lightning as the ICE variants do not have this issue nor do the Mustang Mach Es.

1. Install Ford Q4 Harness into F150 Lightning.
2. Turn on Vehicle and notice no errors
3. Install Comma Power and plug into harness
4. Turn on vehicle and notice no errors
5. Plug in Comma provided USB-C cable to harness (leaving unplugged from Comma 3/3X)
6. Notice the number of errors received (Traction Control, Park Assist, One Pedal Drive)
7. Plug in Comma 3X (with a working release of OpenPilot) and notice issue persist.
8. Allow Openpilot to fully boot and open the relay on the harness and observe the car faults disappearing

### Which car does this affect?

2022+ Ford F150 Lightning

### Provide a route where the issue occurs

N/A - Happens prior to openpilot

### openpilot version

0.9.5

### Additional info

I've spoken with Discord and they're pretty confused on why this is happening as well. Additionally, there's now 2 Lightnings that are observing this behavior -- mine and one other persons.

I've also emailed support and they were a bit confused as well.

hunter_intelligence.technical_hint: Investigate the Ford Q4 Harness initialization sequence and CAN-FD message handling for potential misconfigurations or conflicts when the USB-C cable is connected but OpenPilot is not actively running.
